data.title: Swiss arms export rules to loosen, boosting defense sector
data.description:
Switzerland is moving to ease its restrictive regulations on weapons exports, a change that provides a boost to its domestic defense industry, which has been struggling since the start of Russia's full-scale invasion of Ukraine.

Lawmakers advanced the measure on Thursday, setting the stage for a final parliamentary vote on the amendment later this month. The proposed changes would permit Swiss companies to sell "war material" to a specific roster of 25 nations, primarily Western countries, even if those nations are currently engaged in a conflict. Furthermore, these recipient countries would generally be allowed to re-export the weapons to third parties. The only exclusion for deliveries would be countries judged to be systematic and severe violators of human rights.

The government will, however, maintain the authority to veto any export if it determines that the delivery endangers Swiss neutrality or core foreign and security policy interests.

While Thursday's legislative decision moves the amendment closer to a final approval, its effective date remains uncertain. Lawmakers on the political left have indicated their intent to challenge the new rules by calling for a national referendum.

The war in Ukraine put Swiss defense manufacturers under significant pressure. The country's strict neutrality-based laws prohibit the re-export of war material that is more than 50% Swiss-made. This stance led to multiple European nations barring Swiss firms from defense contracts after Switzerland blocked requests to ship ammunition and arms to Ukraine.

The volume of Swiss war material exports declined for the second consecutive year in 2024, finishing 30% lower than its peak in 2022. Consequently, several Swiss defense companies have begun moving their manufacturing operations outside the country. This shift is a direct response to avoiding the loss of profitable contracts as European states increase their defense budgets and rearm in the wake of Moscow's aggression and persistent U.S. calls for greater European security spending.

data.title: U.S. stocks struggle for direction as Meta rises on Metaverse news, less-than-expected job cuts
data.description:
Wall Street's major averages were ducking underwater on Thursday, while investors assesed Meta Platorms' (META) rise after Mark Zuckerberg reportedly cutting Metaverse spending, and jobless claims came lower than expected.

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was last flat in late morning trade, while the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was -0.1%, and the Dow (DJI) was -0.1%.

Meta Platforms (META) -- last +4.5% -- rose as Bloomberg reported that Mark Zuckerberg said that the company is looking at cutting Metaverse efforts by up to 30%.

On the economic side, the initial jobless claims retreated to below 200K and were better than expected. U.S.-based employers announced 71.32K job cuts in November, down 53% from the 153.07K announced in October.

“Initial claims collapsed last week to a three-year low. The Bureau of Labor Statistics, which compiles this report, did not note any special factors. So, as I highlighted in red all year, the less-than-5-day workweeks distort this series,” said Jim Bianco, president and macro strategist at Bianco Research.

“Last week was Thanksgiving. Previously, these short workweeks resulted in a spike in claims, but Thanksgiving week saw a collapse. Even if you adjust for this week for Thanksgiving, the data still doesn't suggest the Fed should cut rates next week,” he added.

In other news, President Donald Trump's aides were reportedly in talks about appointing Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent as the top White House economic adviser, in addition to his current role, if Kevin Hassett is picked as the next Federal Reserve chair.

Over in the bond market, the 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 2 basis points to 4.09%, while the 2-year yield (US2Y) rose 2 basis points to 3.52%.

data.title: Costco at a crossroads: Strong sales stoke talk of special dividend or the elusive stock split
data.description:
Costco Wholesale Corporation (COST) posted another consistent month of comparable sales growth in November despite concerns around consumer spending, the government shutdown, and a reduction in SNAP payments. 

Net sales for November were $23.64 billion, up 8.1% year over year. Comparable sales rose 6.9%, including 6.0% in the U.S., 6.9% in Canada, and 11.4% in other international markets.  Sales growth by category for November was led by food and sundries, up mid- to high single-digits; fresh foods, up mid- to high single-digits; and non-foods, up mid-single-digits. The ancillary business was up high single digits for the month

UBS analyst Michael Lasser said the firm sees the Costco (COST) results as yet another solid showing by the retail giant. "Furthermore, these results are probably another sign that the consumer is leaning into Costco as it feels the burden of several pressures. We think these bode well for the foreseeable future," he wrote. Overall, UBS believes that COST's strong execution, top-line consistency, and unwavering allegiance to its model help to distinguish it in the retail sector.

Oppenheimer analyst Rupesh Parikh and his team believe that COST's strong execution, top-line consistency, and unwavering allegiance to its model help to distinguish it. "We expect these factors, along with COST's robust sales & traffic metrics, to sustain shares at a premium valuation. Importantly, in a subtle way, the consistency of the comp performance should lessen one of the concerns hanging over the stock, which is the recent degradation in the company’s renewal rate," added Parikh. 

Meanwhile, Evercore ISI analyst Greg Melich said Costco's (COST) fall performance was fairly consistent from a comp perspective, but he observed that on a 2-year view, trends softened as November decelerated 200 basis points from October and October decelerated 150 basis points from September. "While Costco leverages its size & scale to keep prices low and minimize tariff impact for members, the effect of broad-based inflation across retail suggests sluggish volume through fall/winter," updated Melich.

Interestingly, Evercore ISI still sees the potential for a special dividend from Costco (COST) and/or a stock split, which could represent positive catalysts for shares.

For the record, Costco (COST) has executed stock splits in 1991, 1993, and 2000. 

Shares of Costco (COST) fell 2.5% in early Thursday trading. The stock is down 8.5% over the last 52 weeks amid valuation concerns.

data.title: Factory order growth decelerates M/M in September
data.description: U.S. factory orders rose 0.2% M/M to $612.6B in September, decelerating from the prior month's 1.3% rise, which was revised from +1.4% according to data released by the U.S. Census Bureau on Tuesday.

data.title: J.P. Morgan downgrades Fiserv, PayPal; upgrades Toast as part of 2026 outlook
data.description:
J.P. Morgan downgraded Fiserv (FISV) and PayPal (PYPL) (PYPL:CA) to Neutral from Overweight, citing a "too late to sell and too early to buy" view.

"Happy to flush 2025, with payment stocks on track to post their worst performance in 15-years sans COVID, as slower market growth ushered in fears of commoditization and uncertain ROI in new products like lending," said the investment bank in a research note.

For FISV and PYPL, the investment bank said, "2026 feels like a show-me execution year AND an investment year in new initiatives and tech, so a lot can go right, but a lot can disappoint too."

"As such, we prefer a fresh sideline view and expect to learn a lot in 1H to better decide if we want to get back in the game in 2H," said the research note.

Meanwhile, J.P. Morgan upgraded Toast (TOST) to Overweight from Neutral.

"For 2026, we're going back to basics to prefer names with pricing power, strong incremental margins, and front book velocity," said the investment bank.

"We've been eagerly waiting for the right time to take a seat at the Toast table, and with shares down 6% YTD despite estimates up 27%, and Rule of 54% in place for 2026 (or Rule of 59% if you use incremental margin of 38%), plus upside potential if interchange gets regulated, we upgrade shares," said the research note.

data.title: Massimo establishes AI robotics division
data.description:
Massimo Group (MAMO) has established an AI robotics division as part of its long-term roadmap to broaden its technology capabilities and develop new growth avenues.

Massimo AI Technology will serve as a 100% subsidiary of Massimo Group (MAMO), focused on the development of scalable robotic systems that complement the company’s strengths in manufacturing. Initial development areas include industrial automation platforms and logistics and warehouse assistance solutions.

Massimo's robotics programs are currently in early research and development phases, with commercialization timelines to be communicated as progress is achieved.

MAMO shares are down 16.8% at $3.70.

data.title: Broadcom appears set to drive growth due to AI networking, Google chips: Susquehanna
data.description:
Broadcom (AVGO) remains in a solid position to continue driving up revenue growth due to the high demand around artificial intelligence networking and the launch of Google's (GOOG)(GOOGL) Ironwood, or TPUv7 chip, according to Susquehanna.

Broadcom has been a key partner in the design and manufacturing of Google's tensor processing units for about a decade.

"Ironwood is tracking better than expected as Google signed Anthropic (ANTHRO) as a customer earlier this quarter (for up to 1M TPUs), with Meta (META) also reported to be another major customer here," said Susquehanna analysts, led by Christopher Roland, in an investor note. "Last quarter, CEO Hock Tan also announced that one of Broadcom's XPU prospects became a qualified customer (we believe Anthropic), representing over $10B in orders of 'AI racks' expected in 2HFY26 (we note this is likely on the TPU design)."

Susquehanna increased its price target on Broadcom to $450 from $400 and retained its Positive rating.

Susquehanna also highlighted a recent deal with OpenAI (OPENAI) where it has agreed to supply up to 10 gigawatts of AI racks.

data.title: TD Bank Q4 earnings beat as Wealth Management keeps driving growth
data.description:
TD Bank (TD) on Thursday delivered fiscal Q4 earnings that surpassed Wall Street expectations as the lender's Wealth Management arm continued to drive growth.

"TD had a strong fourth quarter, delivering robust fee and trading income in our markets-driven businesses as well as volume growth year-over-year in Canadian Personal and Commercial Banking, capping a year of strong performance," said President and CEO Raymond Chun.

Q4 adjusted EPS of C$2.18 (US$1.56), topping the C$2.01 average analyst estimate, slid from C$2.20 in Q3, but rose from C$1.72 in last year's Q4.

Total adjusted revenue of C$16.0B in the three months ended Oct. 31, 2025, compared with C$15.6B in the quarter ended July 31, 2025, and C$14.9B in the quarter ended Oct. 31, 2025.

TD (TD) shares slipped 0.6% minutes after the opening bell.

Net interest income increased to C$8.55B in Q4 from C$8.53B in the earlier quarter and C$7.94B in Q4 2024.

Adjusted noninterest income was C$8.54B, up from C$8.12B in Q3 and C$7.73B in the year-ago period.

Provision for credit losses rose to C$982M in Q4 from C$971M in Q3, but fell from C$1.11B a year before.

Total loans, net of allowance for losses, were C$953.0B vs. C$936.1B in Q3. Total deposits of C$1.27B in Q4 vs. C$1.26B in Q3.

Adjusted return on common equity fell to 12.8% from 13.2% in Q3, but rose from 11.7% in Q4 2024.

Results by segment: 

  • Canadian Personal & Commercial Banking net income of C$1.87B gained 2% Y/Y, reflecting higher pre-tax, pre-provision earnings, partially offset by higher provisions for credit losses.
  • U.S. Retail net income of C$719M climbed 31% Y/Y, excluding contributions of C$154M in Q4 2024 from TD's (TD) investment in Charles Schwab (SCHW).
  • Wealth Management & Insurance net income of C$699M surged by C$350M from a year earlier, driven by record earnings in Wealth Management and lower losses from catastrophe claims in Insurance.
  • Wholesale Banking net income of C$494M jumped by C$259M Y/Y, mainly reflecting higher revenue and lower PCL, partially offset by higher non-interest expenses.
